  <h2 id="lecture_heading" class="section-title dsp-flex-xs flex-align-items-center-xs" data-lecture-id="29475297" data-next-lecture-id="30094455" data-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/29475297" data-next-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/30094455" ,="" data-previous-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/31212744" data-previous-lecture-id="31212744">

    3. State of Mind: Level 4
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=51701545, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-51701545">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Ok so, is everything going as fast as you want it to go?</p><p>Or do you still need to get your "state of mind" right?</p><p>Here are the levels in ascending order (I'll be going in detail in upcoming modules):</p><h1><strong>State of mind: Level 1.</strong></h1><p>Narrowed goals. I know your ego wants you to be a musician / painter / poet / director. And you WILL be all of those things one day. But for now, become GREAT at one thing. Build a strong audience with it. THEN switch.</p><h1><strong>State of mind: Level 2</strong></h1><p>Focused.</p><p>Your phone? The enemy. Bingeing shows? The enemy. The current news cycle? The enemy.</p><p>Time to focus.</p><h1><strong>State of mind: Level 3</strong></h1><p>Consistency is queen. Only when you're focused can you be consistent.</p><p>Drop bombs more often.</p><p>But there is one state of mind that I hardly get to talk about.</p><p>Because most people are still trying climb the first 3.</p><p>It's my favorite one. </p><p>Here it is if you're ready for it.</p><h1><strong>State of Mind: Level 4</strong></h1><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">It's tempting to separate our lives into neat little boxes.</span></p><p>Creative time. Time to hang out with friends. Work. Alone time etc.</p><p>But the reality is...</p><p>If you're creative, that creativity is always bubbling under and waiting to be expressed. </p><p>Creativity is a perpetual motion machine.</p><p>That's why you get amazing ideas at the weirdest times.</p><p>It's always below the surface. Working away...</p><p>But armed with this knowledge, there are two things you can do to maximize more frequent appearances of the muse in your life.</p><p>One of them you're already doing.</p><p>You learned it in the last module:</p><h1><strong>1. Daily Practice (which gives you the "Zeigarnik Effect" that everyone wants).</strong></h1><p>But the other way to unleash your creativity's full power is to get rid of the boxes.</p><h1><strong>2. Start looking at every moment you've scheduled in your day as a part of one continuous creative flow.</strong></h1><p>It may not feel natural at first. And some moments will be downright awkward.</p><p>"Father Bronques... You want me to see even my fights with my boyfriend as part of my creative flow? And even when I'm going for a shit? Like, how far are we taking this here?"</p><p>All the way.</p><p>If you want to improve your artistic output quality, change your relationship to everything you used to call mundane and undesirable.</p><p>Obsessive?</p><p>Maybe.</p><p>Observe your heroes. Read their biographies. Watch their documentaries. How did they behave on their ascent?</p><p>Imagine how much energy you'd have if you stopped judging everything.</p><p>Imagine how your mind would relax when you stop being at war with your schedule. And started seeing everything as connected instead.</p><p>Some of it is "maintenance". Some are activities you can't delegate to an assistant. Like visiting your grandmother... Or eating... Or going to the gym.</p><p>Activities you'll say are "stealing time away from your art"?</p><p>When the truth is...</p><p>What you do when you're NOT being creative has more influence on your art than anything else.</p><p>So why not treat it with that kind of respect? </p><p>People have it half right when they say stuff like, "I need to 'go live a little' so I have something to say in my art."</p><p>But where they have it wrong is that they think they need to travel or <em>suffer trauma</em>. No, no. Chill out. </p><p>You're experiencing things every day.</p><p>You just haven't been paying attention to the nuances.</p><p>Remember that funny scene in the Ray Charles movie?</p><p>The one where he's is fighting with his girl about "keeping the baby"? And she starts crying. And he's yelling. But suddenly, he realizes that this moment contains a bottled essence of great emotion... So he starts singing...</p><p>"Hit The Road Jack..."</p><p>And he starts playing the piano and writes the song.</p><p>Even she starts singing! Mad as hell and crying, she STILL understands:</p><p>You can't stop creativity.</p><p>So yeah, I DO want you to see your "fights with your boyfriend" as part of your creative flow from now on.</p><p>That and everything else.</p><p>(I remember a student taking two weeks off because their relationship was on the rocks. What?)</p><p>So let's imagine that from now on, activities like going to the gym will no longer be called "going to the gym"...</p><p>Rename it. Call it something like "exercising mental fortitude for my next deep dive in my writing".</p><p>Give care and attention to activities like flossing your teeth and doing your taxes.</p><p>The reward?</p><p>More energy in the totality of your existence. More flow. More art.</p><p>You will become a perpetual motion machine.</p><p>If you've never considered this, it may be uncomfortable to hear the first time around.</p><p>Maybe it challenges your whole belief system.</p><p>If so... Ignore it for now. </p><p>Just make a mental note for later when you're at an important crossroad soul-searching.</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21444354" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Continue here to the next module if you like... </a></p><p>But...</p><p>If you've had similar thoughts already...</p><p>And all you needed was validation, or "permission", or a sign to dive right in... </p><p><sounds of angels and trumpets></p><p>The old way is over.</p><p>"Leading an uninspired day and expecting great art to emerge" is a construct of the past. </p><p>That used to work just fine:</p><p>You wrestled with your mind-- and eventually art came out. </p><p>But I want you to be more than "fine" for you.</p><p>"Imagine what it would look like if it were easy."</p><p><strong>There's a whole new world waiting to show up at your creative doorstep when you start looking at the entire picture of your life as ONE LONG FLOW ACTIVITY.</strong></p><p>Are you ready to adopt this? Start right after you read this. You'll feel the change immediately. So will everyone around you.</p><p>It's that powerful.</p><p>Inject creativity in every "in-between" moment of your day.</p><p>No matter how awkward.</p><p>Even if the normals call you "eccentric".</p><p>And let your language reflect the changes. Other Level 4 creatives will understand you right away.</p><p><em>(These sentences are all taken from real life. From famous people who's work you consume. From people I know you admire. So shhhhhh.):</em></p><p>"Everything I do, I do it for art. There's no point in the day where I'm not trying to sneak it in there."</p><p>"I only sleep so I have more energy and ideas. And the only reason I eat is so I don't crash when all my best ideas start coming."</p><p>"I wake up at 4 am. I drink water, but I don't eat. I meditate. I know it doesn't sound creative. But without it, I'm useless."</p><p>"If I didn't walk my dogs every morning, I wouldn't have written (hit song)."</p><p><em>You get the idea.</em></p><p>Now, how about you?</p><p><em>What "crazy" things will come out of your mouth when you start living every moment like they're scenes from a movie? </em></p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

    <div class="comment-content">
      <div data-original-comment-body="My most creative time ever was when my ex and I split in summer of 2021. That breakup stripped me of all of my possessions, including my home. I was homeless from August - December and crashed friends couches, slept in offices, Airbnbs, hotels while finishing grad school. 

Somehow I found the energy to go to the dance studio to train to get through that pain. I utilized my gym to crosstrain daily. I came up with my best choreography in those months and leveled up as an athlete. I was in the flow in spite of what i was going through. The energy I got in finding my creativity in dance again was directly related to admitting my dignity mattered more than my ex's opinion of what I should do with my career and life. 

Then, in 2022, i got comfortable in my own apartment, started paying massive bills, and began to sit on my ass all day applying to corporate jobs in design. 

I am still trying to figure out what all of this means but I know I lost my flow and I'm split so many ways, missing doing the thing I love that doesn't pay enough, putting time into applying for design jobs so that i can get steady paid, and dancing at clubs to stay afloat in between. I'm split and exhausted. The creativity for dance is dry. I am nowhere near being creative as a designer as the kind of design I do is research design and it is the most boring shit ever. 

I miss dancing but it seems like I made a choice to get into design so I can get financial stability. I dream of having my own studio someday so I can dance again.
